name: Pepper Steak (Another Version)
recipeIngredient: 1 can (8 oz) sliced water chestnuts, drained
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut steak into 2-inch by 1/8-inch strips.
2. Heat oil in a large skillet or wok. Brown steak for about 5 minutes, turning occasionally, until browned on all sides but not cooked through.
3. Remove the meat and set aside, keeping warm.
4. Add garlic, ginger, salt (if using), and pepper to the skillet. Cook for 1 minute, stirring frequently.
5. Add green peppers and onions to the skillet. Stir-fry for about 5 minutes, or until the vegetables are crisp-tender.
6. Dissolve beef bouillon granules in hot water. Pour this mixture into the skillet with the water chestnuts.
7. In a small bowl, whisk together cornstarch, soy sauce, cold water, and sugar to make a slurry.
8. Add the meat back to the skillet. Stir in the slurry until it thickens slightly.
9. Cook for 2 more minutes, stirring frequently, until the sauce has cooked through and coats the meat and vegetables.
